questions.text: Where is the respiratory center located?
Which nerve carries signals from the medullary inspiratory area to the diaphragm?
What spinal nerve roots contribute to the phrenic nerve?
During quiet breathing, approximately how long does the inspiratory area fire before impulses stop?
What happens when the impulses from the inspiratory area stop during quiet breathing?

answers.text: Expiratory area activates the abdominal muscles

questions.text: What is the role of the expiratory area during quiet breathing?

answers.text: It fires continuously to maintain exhalation

questions.text: During forceful breathing, which muscles are stimulated by the expiratory area?
What is the primary function of the pneumotaxic center?

answers.text: Coordinate the transition from inhalation to exhalation
Detect changes in blood oxygen levels

questions.text: Where is the pneumotaxic center located?
Increased activity of the pneumotaxic center results in what change to breathing?
What is the function of the apneustic center?

answers.text: Send stimulatory impulses to the inspiratory area

questions.text: What prevents the apneustic center from causing overinflation of the lungs?
What eventually overrides voluntary breath holding?

answers.text: Low oxygen levels detected by peripheral chemoreceptors
CO₂ and H⁺ buildup stimulating chemoreceptors
Decreased blood pressure in the carotid arteries

questions.text: Where are central chemoreceptors located?
What do central chemoreceptors primarily respond to?

answers.text: H⁺ concentration or PCO₂ in CSF

questions.text: Where are peripheral chemoreceptors located?
What are the carotid bodies?

answers.text: Oval nodules in the common carotid arteries
Lymph nodes near the carotid artery

questions.text: Which of the following do peripheral chemoreceptors detect?
What happens when peripheral chemoreceptors detect oxygen deficiency?

answers.text: Respiratory rate decreases to conserve oxygen
Rate and depth of breathing increase

questions.text: What stimulates proprioceptors to increase ventilation at the onset of exercise?

answers.text: Movement detected in muscles, tendons, and joints

questions.text: When does proprioceptor stimulation of breathing occur relative to chemoreceptor changes?
Which equation underlies CO₂ transport and chemoreceptor regulation?
The majority of CO₂ is transported in the blood as which form?
Damage to the pneumotaxic center with an intact apneustic center would likely produce which breathing pattern?
Which of the following is true about the cortical influence on breathing?

answers.text: The cortex cannot override the brainstem rhythm at all
Breath holding can be sustained indefinitely with practice
The cortex can voluntarily alter breathing but is limited by chemoreceptor drive
The cortex only affects breathing during sleep
